truthAudit.Summary: The audit of Run 2 against the ground truth reveals that the assistant correctly identified major buffer overflow vulnerabilities (SC-V3-005, SC-V3-009), administrative credential security issues (SC-V3-010), and audit log manipulation issues (SC-V3-013), but missed exactly 14 out of 20 classified true vulnerabilities, including critical logic-heavy bugs like the Use-After-Free in memory management (SC-V3-003) and the integer overflow in factorial processing (SC-V3-002).

Items.Notes: Previous answer correctly identified the stack buffer overflow situation occurring when expression length equals or exceeds the 1024-byte buffer size in the evaluate_expression function.
Previous answer identified the heap-based buffer overflow in the expression parser where expressions are copied into the 256-byte expression_buffer_.
Previous answer correctly identified the hardcoded administrative secret assigned to ADMIN_SECRET.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote:
const char command = "sh -c \"echo Admin override logged >> " + audit_path + "\"";
system(command.c_str());
Items.Notes: Previous answer identified the insecure handling of temporary file paths in audit logging, relying on user-controlled environment variables (TMPDIR) through system(), which matches the ground truth.
